FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms between a Client and a Freelancer for developing a web application for the Client's internal HR management system. It details the specific components to be built, payment structure, intellectual property rights, confidentiality, and conditions for termination. The project is structured into four phases, with clear inclusions and exclusions for the scope of work.

payment.summary: The total project compensation is INR 2,00,000, excluding taxes. Payments are milestone-based and due within 15 days of invoice and deliverable approval. Late payments incur a 1.5% monthly penalty. Pre-approved third-party expenses are reimbursed separately. The Freelancer is responsible for income tax, while the Client will handle TDS if applicable, and GST will be charged extra.

work_scope.summary: The Freelancer will design, develop, test, and deliver specific web application components, including a custom employee dashboard, RESTful API integration, React.js UI/UX, real-time notifications, PDF report generation, an admin panel, and a comprehensive test suite. Excluded items are mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, post-launch support beyond any warranty, and hardware costs.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created for the project become the Client's property as 'work made for hire'. The Freelancer retains rights to general-purpose tools, anonymized portfolio display, and pre-existing proprietary code. Open-source components will be listed in a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) with their respective license obligations.

deadlines.summary: The project is to be executed in four phases. Any time extensions caused by Client delays in feedback or approvals will not count against the Freelancer.
deadlines.risk: Deadline risk: The actual project timeline and specific milestones for each of the four phases are not provided in the document text, which is a critical omission for project planning and tracking.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' written notice. If the Client terminates, they must pay for all work completed up to that date on a pro-rata basis. Immediate termination is possible for material breach (if not cured within 14 days), insolvency, bankruptcy, fraud, or willful misconduct. Upon termination, completed work is delivered, and ownership transfers upon full payment of outstanding invoices.

risks.title: Missing Project Timeline and Milestones
risks.explanation: The document states the project will be executed in four phases but does not provide the actual timeline, specific milestones, or durations. This makes it impossible to track progress, set expectations, or manage the project effectively.

risks.title: Ambiguity in Deliverable Approval Criteria
risks.explanation: Payments are contingent on 'approving the corresponding deliverable'. Without clear, objective acceptance criteria, disputes may arise regarding whether a deliverable meets the required standards for approval.

risks.title: Incomplete Bank Details
risks.explanation: The agreement references 'Schedule A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT' but the actual bank details are not included in the provided document text. This is an administrative oversight that needs to be rectified before payments can be made.

suggestions: Request the complete 'PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES' table from Section 3, including specific dates, durations, and deliverables for each of the four phases.
Define clear and objective acceptance criteria for each deliverable to avoid disputes during the 'approving the corresponding deliverable' stage.
Ensure 'Schedule A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT' is fully completed and attached to the final agreement.

Consider adding a clause for a limited warranty period for bug fixes post-delivery, as 'Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period' is explicitly out of scope, implying a warranty period might be beneficial to define.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, ensure the complete project timeline with specific milestones and acceptance criteria for deliverables is fully documented and agreed upon. Confirm all financial details, including bank information and tax implications, are clearly understood and attached. Address the missing timeline information as it is crucial for project success.
analysis.confidence_note: The document text was incomplete as the 'PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES' table (Section 3) was missing, which is crucial for project planning. Also, 'Schedule A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT' was referenced but not included.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms for a Freelancer to develop a web application for a Client's internal HR system. It covers the specific deliverables, payment schedule, intellectual property rights, confidentiality, and termination conditions. Key aspects include a total compensation of INR 2,00,000, a 15-day payment window with late penalties, and the Client owning the custom work upon full payment. The document also details what is excluded from the scope and how changes will be handled.

payment.summary: The total payment for the project is INR 2,00,000, excluding taxes. Payments are due within 15 days of receiving an invoice for each completed milestone. Late payments will incur a 1.5% monthly penalty, and the Freelancer can suspend work if invoices are overdue. Pre-approved expenses will be reimbursed separately. The Freelancer is responsible for their income taxes, and the Client will handle TDS if applicable.
payment.risk: Payment risk: Client faces a 1.5% monthly late fee (18% annually) for overdue payments and the Freelancer can suspend work, potentially delaying the project. Client is also responsible for transaction charges and must ensure timely approval of deliverables to avoid payment delays.

work_scope.summary: The Freelancer will develop specific web application components for an internal HR system, including a custom employee dashboard, API integration, responsive UI/UX, real-time notifications, PDF report generation, an admin panel, comprehensive testing (80% code coverage), and AWS deployment scripts. Excluded items are mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, ongoing post-launch support beyond warranty, and hardware costs.
work_scope.risk: Scope risk: Items explicitly listed as 'Out of Scope' (e.g., mobile apps, payroll integrations, ongoing support) will require a separate agreement and additional costs if needed, which could lead to project delays or budget overruns if not anticipated by the Client.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created for the project become the Client's property as 'work made for hire'. The Freelancer retains rights to general-purpose tools, portfolio display of anonymized work, and pre-existing proprietary code. The project may use open-source components, and the Freelancer will provide a list of these, with the Client acknowledging their license obligations.
ownership.risk: Ownership risk: The Client must understand and comply with the license obligations of any open-source components used in the project, as listed in the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM). Failure to do so could lead to compliance issues.

deadlines.summary: The project is planned in four phases, but the specific timeline and milestones table is not provided in this document. Client delays in providing feedback or approvals will not count against the Freelancer's timeline.
deadlines.risk: Deadline risk: The specific 'Project Timeline & Milestones' table is missing from the document, which creates uncertainty regarding exact delivery dates for each phase. Client delays in feedback or approvals can extend the project timeline without penalty to the Freelancer.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can end the agreement with 30 days' written notice. If the Client terminates, they must pay for all work completed up to that date on a pro-rata basis. Either party can terminate immediately for a material breach (if not fixed within 14 days), insolvency, bankruptcy, fraud, or willful misconduct. Upon termination, the Freelancer delivers completed work, and ownership transfers once all outstanding invoices are paid. Work in progress remains the Freelancer's property until payment.
ending_terms.risk: Ending terms risk: If the Client terminates the agreement, they must pay for all work completed. Work in progress will remain the Freelancer's property until all outstanding invoices are fully paid, which could delay the Client's access to partially completed deliverables.

risks.title: Undefined Project Timeline
risks.explanation: The specific 'Project Timeline & Milestones' table is missing, leading to ambiguity regarding exact delivery dates for each phase. This could cause planning difficulties for the Client.

risks.title: Late Payment Penalties and Work Suspension
risks.explanation: The Client will incur a 1.5% monthly late fee on overdue payments, and the Freelancer has the right to suspend work. This can lead to increased costs and significant project delays.

risks.title: Limited Freelancer Liability
risks.explanation: The Freelancer's total liability is capped at the fees paid in the preceding three months. This means the Client may not be fully compensated for larger losses or damages resulting from the Freelancer's actions or omissions.

risks.title: Scope Misunderstanding for Excluded Items
risks.explanation: Items explicitly 'Out of Scope' (e.g., mobile apps, ongoing support) will require separate agreements and costs. If the Client assumes these are included, it could lead to unexpected expenses and project scope changes.

risks.title: Open Source License Obligations
risks.explanation: The Client must understand and comply with the license obligations of any open-source components used in the project. Failure to do so could lead to legal or compliance issues.

risks.title: Non-Solicitation Clause
risks.explanation: The Client is restricted from directly hiring any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for 12 months after the agreement ends, limiting future hiring flexibility.

suggestions: Ensure the detailed 'Project Timeline & Milestones' table, including specific dates for each phase and deliverable, is fully defined and attached to the agreement before signing.
Establish a clear internal process for timely review and 'approving the corresponding deliverable' to avoid payment delays and late fees.
Carefully review the 'Out of Scope' items to confirm all necessary features are covered, or plan for separate agreements for those items if they are critical.
Understand the implications of the Freelancer's limited liability and consider if additional protections are needed for potential larger losses.
Obtain and review the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for open-source components early to understand and comply with all license obligations.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, ensure all missing details, especially the specific project timeline and milestones, are fully documented and agreed upon. Clearly understand the payment terms, late penalties, and the scope of work, including what is explicitly excluded. Pay close attention to the Freelancer's limited liability and the implications of open-source licenses. content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms for a freelancer to design, develop, test, and deliver specific web application components for a client's internal HR management system. It details the scope of work, payment structure, intellectual property rights, and conditions for termination. The project is intended to be executed in phases, with provisions for revisions and change requests.

payment.summary: The total compensation is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of taxes. Payments are due within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving an invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable for each milestone. Late payments incur a 1.5% monthly penalty (18% per annum), and the Freelancer can suspend work for overdue invoices. Pre-approved third-party expenses are reimbursed separately. The Freelancer is responsible for income taxes, and the Client will handle TDS if applicable, issuing Form 16A. GST will be charged additionally.
payment.risk: High. The Client faces significant late payment penalties (1.5% per month) and the risk of work suspension if invoices are not paid within the 15-day window. This could lead to project delays and increased costs.

work_scope.summary: The Freelancer will develop a custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access, RESTful API integration, responsive UI/UX using React.js and Tailwind CSS, a real-time notification system, PDF report generation, an Admin panel with CRUD operations, comprehensive unit and integration tests (minimum 80% coverage), and deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker. Explicitly excluded are mobile app development, third-party payroll gateway integrations, ongoing post-launch technical support beyond warranty, and hardware/server infrastructure costs.
work_scope.risk: Low. The scope is clearly defined with explicit exclusions, which minimizes the risk of scope creep or misunderstandings. Any changes to this scope require a formal written Change Order.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created for this Project will be assigned to the Client as 'work made for hire,' giving the Client all rights, title, and interest. The Freelancer retains rights to use general-purpose libraries, frameworks, pre-existing proprietary tools, and to display anonymized project screenshots in a portfolio. Open-source components may be used, and the Freelancer will provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM), with the Client acknowledging their respective license obligations.
ownership.risk: Medium. Ownership transfer is contingent on 'full payment for each Phase.' If payments are incomplete or disputed, the Client may not fully own the work. The Client also needs to be aware of and manage obligations related to open-source licenses.

deadlines.summary: The Project is to be executed in four (4) phases. However, the specific timeline and milestones for these phases are not provided in the document text. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals will not count against the Freelancer.
deadlines.risk: High. The critical 'PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES' table, which would detail the specific deadlines for each of the four phases, is missing from the provided document. This makes it impossible to assess the project schedule, potential delays, or track progress effectively.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can terminate the agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice (termination for convenience). Either party can also terminate immediately for cause, such as a material breach not cured within 14 days, insolvency, bankruptcy, fraud, or willful misconduct. Upon termination, the Freelancer must deliver all completed work, and ownership transfers to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress remains the Freelancer's property until paid.
ending_terms.risk: Medium. If the Client terminates for convenience, they are still obligated to pay for all work completed up to the termination date. If termination occurs before full payment, any work in progress will remain the Freelancer's property, potentially hindering the Client's ability to continue the project with another party.

risks.title: Missing Project Timeline Details
risks.explanation: The document states the project has four phases but does not provide the actual timeline or specific milestones for these phases, which is crucial for project planning, tracking, and managing expectations.

risks.title: Late Payment Penalties and Work Suspension
risks.explanation: The Client will incur significant late fees (1.5% per month) and risks the Freelancer suspending work if invoices are not paid within 15 days, potentially leading to project delays and increased costs.

risks.title: Conditional IP Ownership Transfer
risks.explanation: Ownership of deliverables transfers only upon 'full payment for each Phase.' If payments are incomplete or disputed, the Client may not fully own the work, creating potential legal and operational issues.

risks.title: Open Source License Obligations
risks.explanation: The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations, which need to be understood and managed to ensure compliance and avoid future legal issues.

risks.title: Limited Liability for Freelancer
risks.explanation: The Freelancer's total liability is capped at the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three months, which may limit the Client's recourse in case of significant issues or damages caused by the Freelancer.

suggestions: **Crucially, obtain and review the complete 'PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES' table** mentioned in Section 3 to understand specific deadlines for each phase and ensure it aligns with your project needs.
Establish a clear internal process for timely feedback, approvals, and invoice payments to avoid late fees and potential project suspension.
Review the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for open-source components to understand and comply with all associated license obligations.
Ensure any changes to the project scope are formally documented via a Change Order, understanding that this will impact cost and timeline.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, ensure the missing project timeline and milestone details are fully provided and understood. Confirm your internal processes can meet the payment terms to avoid penalties and work suspension. Review the scope and exclusions carefully, and be prepared for formal change orders for any modifications. Pay close attention to the conditions for IP ownership transfer.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms for a freelancer to develop a web application for a client's internal HR management system. It details the specific work scope, payment structure of INR 2,00,000, intellectual property ownership, and conditions for contract termination. The document also covers confidentiality, revision processes, and dispute resolution, aiming for a clear understanding between both parties.

payment.summary: The total project compensation is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), excluding taxes. Payments will be made in Indian Rupees via bank transfer upon completion and approval of each project milestone, within 15 calendar days of invoicing. Late payments will incur a 1.5% monthly penalty. Pre-approved third-party expenses will be reimbursed separately. The freelancer is responsible for income taxes, while the client handles TDS if applicable, and GST will be charged additionally.
payment.risk: The payment terms are clearly defined, including late payment penalties. The primary risk lies with the client for potential late payments, which could lead to additional fees and work suspension. For the freelancer, the risk is cash flow disruption if payments are delayed.

work_scope.summary: The freelancer will design, develop, test, and deliver specific web application components for the client's HR system. This includes a custom employee dashboard with role-based access, RESTful API integration, responsive UI/UX using React.js, a real-time notification system, PDF report generation, an admin panel for user management, and comprehensive unit/integration tests. Explicitly excluded are mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, ongoing post-launch support, and hardware costs.
work_scope.risk: The scope of work is well-defined with clear inclusions and exclusions, which significantly reduces the risk of 'scope creep' or misunderstandings. The term 'minor revisions' could be a slight point of contention if not clearly defined.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created specifically for this project will be assigned to the client as 'work made for hire,' granting the client full ownership. The freelancer retains rights to use general-purpose libraries, pre-existing tools, and to display anonymized project details in their professional portfolio. The client acknowledges that open-source components used will carry their respective license obligations.
ownership.risk: Ownership terms are clear and standard, ensuring the client gains full intellectual property rights upon payment. The freelancer's retained rights are reasonable and common. Risk is low.

deadlines.summary: The project is planned to be executed in four phases. Any delays caused by the client's feedback or approval process will not count against the freelancer's timeline. However, the specific details of the project timeline and milestones, such as dates or durations for each phase, are not provided in this document text.
deadlines.risk: High risk. The actual project timeline and specific milestone dates are missing from the provided document text. This lack of defined deadlines is a significant risk for project planning, tracking, and overall execution, potentially leading to delays and disputes.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 calendar days' written notice. If the client terminates, they must pay for all work completed up to the termination date on a pro-rata basis. Immediate termination is possible if the other party materially breaches the agreement (with a 14-day cure period), becomes insolvent, files for bankruptcy, commits fraud, or engages in willful misconduct. Upon termination, the freelancer must deliver all completed work, with ownership transferring to the client upon full payment of outstanding invoices.
ending_terms.risk: The termination terms are comprehensive and clearly outline the conditions and financial implications for both parties, whether termination is for convenience or cause. Risk is low.

risks.title: Missing Project Timeline Details
risks.explanation: Section 3 mentions a 'Project Timeline & Milestones' table, but the actual details (phases, dates, durations) are not included in the document. This leaves critical project deadlines undefined, making planning and tracking difficult and increasing the potential for delays.

risks.title: Subjectivity of 'Minor Revisions'
risks.explanation: Each milestone includes 'up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions.' The term 'minor' can be subjective and lead to disagreements if not clearly defined, potentially causing delays or additional costs if expectations differ.

risks.title: Client Liability for Damages
risks.explanation: The freelancer's total liability under this agreement is capped at the total fees paid by the client in the preceding three months. This means the client bears significant financial risk for any damages or losses that exceed this cap, which could be substantial in a software project.

risks.title: Open Source License Management
risks.explanation: The project may incorporate open-source components, and the client acknowledges their respective license obligations. While standard, the client needs to ensure they understand and comply with these licenses to avoid potential legal issues related to software distribution or modification.

suggestions: Ensure the 'Project Timeline & Milestones' table (Section 3) is fully completed and agreed upon, detailing specific dates and deliverables for each phase, before signing.
Clarify what constitutes 'minor revisions' (Section 7.1) with specific examples or limits to avoid disputes during the revision process.
Establish a clear process and timeline for client approval of deliverables (Section 4.2) to prevent delays in payment and project progress.
Review the Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for open-source components (Section 5.3) and understand any associated license obligations to ensure compliance.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, it is crucial to ensure that all critical project details, especially the complete project timeline and milestone dates, are explicitly defined and agreed upon. Clarify the scope of 'minor revisions' to prevent future disagreements. Fully understand the payment schedule, late payment penalties, and the process for expense reimbursement. Pay close attention to the intellectual property terms, ensuring full ownership transfer upon payment. Given the limitation of liability, the client should be aware of their potential exposure to risks beyond the freelancer's capped liability.
analysis.confidence_note: The document text was incomplete regarding the 'Project Timeline & Milestones' (Section 3), which is a critical missing piece of information for project execution and risk assessment.

Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms for a freelancer to develop a web application for a client's internal HR management system. It details the specific components to be built, payment structure, intellectual property ownership, confidentiality, and termination conditions. The project is divided into four phases, with payment tied to milestone completion.

payment.summary: The total compensation is INR 2,00,000, excluding taxes. Payments are due within 15 calendar days of receiving an invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable for each milestone. Late payments incur a 1.5% monthly penalty, and the freelancer can suspend work. Pre-approved expenses are reimbursed, and the client is responsible for TDS if applicable, with GST charged additionally.
payment.risk: Payment risk: High. The client faces a significant late payment penalty (1.5% per month) and the risk of project suspension if payments are not made within 15 days. Client is also responsible for applicable taxes (TDS, GST) which needs clear understanding and compliance.

work_scope.summary: The freelancer will design, develop, test, and deliver specific web application components for an internal HR system, including a custom employee dashboard, RESTful API, React.js UI/UX, real-time notifications, PDF report generation, admin panel, and a comprehensive test suite (80% code coverage). Explicitly excluded are mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, ongoing post-launch support, and hardware costs.
work_scope.risk: Scope risk: Low. The scope of work and exclusions are clearly defined, which reduces the risk of misunderstandings. However, any changes to this scope will require a formal written Change Order, potentially impacting costs and timelines.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each project phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created for this project become the Client's property as 'work made for hire'. The Freelancer retains rights to use general-purpose libraries, pre-existing tools, and to display anonymized project screenshots in their portfolio. Open-source components are subject to their respective licenses.
ownership.risk: Ownership risk: Medium. Ownership transfer is contingent on 'full payment' for each phase. If payments are delayed or incomplete, the client may not fully own the work, which could lead to disputes. The client also needs to be aware of and comply with open-source license obligations.

deadlines.summary: The project is structured into four phases. The document states that time extensions due to client delays in feedback or approvals will not count against the freelancer. However, specific dates, durations, or a detailed timeline for these phases are not provided in the document text.
deadlines.risk: Deadline risk: High. The document mentions a 'Project Timeline & Milestones' section with four phases but does not provide the actual timeline details (e.g., start/end dates, durations for each phase). This lack of specific deadlines creates significant ambiguity and potential for project delays or disputes if not clarified.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' written notice. If the Client terminates, they must pay for all work completed up to that date on a pro-rata basis. Immediate termination is possible for material breach (if not cured within 14 days), insolvency, fraud, or willful misconduct. Upon termination, completed work transfers to the Client only after all outstanding invoices are paid.
ending_terms.risk: Ending terms risk: Medium. If the client terminates for convenience, they are still liable for work completed, which could be a substantial cost. Crucially, ownership of completed deliverables only transfers upon payment of *all outstanding invoices*, meaning work-in-progress remains the freelancer's property if payments are not fully settled at termination.

risks.title: Undefined Project Timeline
risks.explanation: Section 3 mentions four project phases but lacks specific dates, durations, or a detailed timeline table. This ambiguity can lead to project delays, disputes over progress, and difficulty in planning for the client.

risks.title: Payment-Dependent IP Transfer
risks.explanation: Ownership of custom code and designs only transfers to the Client upon 'full payment' for each phase. If payments are delayed or incomplete, the Client may not fully own the work, creating legal and operational risks.

risks.title: Late Payment Penalties and Work Suspension
risks.explanation: The contract imposes a significant late payment penalty (1.5% per month) and allows the Freelancer to suspend work if invoices are not paid within 15 days. This could severely impact project progress and incur additional costs for the Client.

risks.title: Limited Liability for Freelancer
risks.explanation: The Freelancer's total liability is capped at the fees paid in the preceding three months, and neither party is liable for indirect or consequential damages. This limits the Client's financial recourse in case of significant issues caused by the Freelancer.

suggestions: Request a detailed project timeline with specific start and end dates for each of the four phases mentioned in Section 3, including clear deliverables and acceptance criteria for each milestone.
Clarify the process for client feedback and approvals, including defined response times, to prevent delays that might impact the project timeline.
Review internal payment processes to ensure invoices can be paid within the 15-day window to avoid late payment penalties and potential project suspension.
Ensure a clear understanding of the open-source licenses used and their implications for the Client's future use and distribution of the software.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, ensure a detailed project timeline with specific dates for each phase is clearly defined and attached as an addendum. Confirm your internal payment processes can consistently meet the 15-day payment window to avoid penalties and project halts. Have a clear understanding of the scope exclusions and the process for change requests to manage expectations and budget effectively.
analysis.confidence_note: The document text was incomplete regarding the detailed project timeline (Section 3 mentions a table but its content is missing), which impacts the clarity of deadlines and project planning.

content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.document_title: Freelance Services Agreement: Web App Development
analysis.overview: This Freelance Services Agreement outlines the terms for a Freelancer to design, develop, test, and deliver specific web application components for a Client's internal HR management system. It details the scope of work, a total compensation of INR 2,00,000, payment schedule, intellectual property rights, confidentiality obligations, and terms for revisions, termination, and dispute resolution.
analysis.eli15_summary: This is a deal where a freelance developer will build a special part of a website for a company's HR system. The company will pay INR 2,00,000 in stages, and once paid, the company will own the new website code. Both sides promise to keep secrets, and there are clear rules for making changes, ending the project, and what to do if there's a disagreement.
analysis.duration_note: Defined by milestones, no fixed overall contract duration specified.

key_clauses.title: Total Compensation
key_clauses.description: The total amount the Client will pay the Freelancer for the entire project, excluding taxes.

key_clauses.title: Payment Due Date
key_clauses.description: Client must make payments within 15 calendar days of receiving an invoice for completed milestones.

key_clauses.title: Intellectual Property
key_clauses.description: Upon full payment for each phase, the Client owns all custom code and designs created for the project.

key_clauses.title: Confidentiality Period
key_clauses.description: Both parties must keep confidential information secret for three years after the agreement ends.

key_clauses.title: Liability Limitation
key_clauses.description: The Freelancer's total liability is capped at the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three months.

key_clauses.title: Late Payment Penalty
key_clauses.description: Overdue payments will incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum).

key_clauses.title: Termination Notice
key_clauses.description: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 calendar days' written notice.

key_clauses.title: Dispute Resolution
key_clauses.description: Disputes are first negotiated, then resolved through binding arbitration in Bangalore, India.

payment.summary: The total project compensation is INR 2,00,000, paid in Indian Rupees via bank transfer upon completion of each milestone. Invoices must be paid within 15 days. Pre-approved expenses are reimbursed separately. The Client is responsible for applicable TDS and GST.
payment.risk: High. A significant late payment penalty of 1.5% per month (18% annually) applies to overdue payments, and the Freelancer can suspend work. Client is responsible for bank transaction charges and TDS.

work_scope.summary: The Freelancer will develop specific web application components including a custom employee dashboard, RESTful API, React.js UI/UX, real-time notifications, PDF report generation, an admin panel, and comprehensive testing. Mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, and ongoing post-launch support are explicitly excluded.
work_scope.risk: Low. The scope is clearly defined with specific deliverables and explicit exclusions, minimizing ambiguity.

ownership.summary: Upon full payment for each phase, the Client will own all custom code, designs, and documentation created for the project. The Freelancer retains rights to general-purpose tools, pre-existing IP, and the ability to display anonymized project work in their portfolio. Open-source components are subject to their respective licenses.
ownership.risk: Low. Intellectual property transfer is clearly defined, and standard retained rights for the freelancer are outlined. The provision for a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for open-source components is good practice.

deadlines.summary: The project is structured into four phases, with specific milestones. Client delays in feedback or approvals will not count against the Freelancer. Payment for invoices is due within 15 calendar days of receipt.
deadlines.risk: Medium. While phased, the document does not specify overall project completion dates or detailed timelines for each phase within the provided text, which could lead to ambiguities if not detailed elsewhere. The 15-day payment window is standard, but the late fee is high.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' written notice, with the Client paying for all work completed up to the termination date. Immediate termination is possible for material breach (uncured in 14 days), insolvency, fraud, or willful misconduct. Upon termination, completed work transfers to the Client once all outstanding invoices are paid.
ending_terms.risk: Low. Termination clauses are standard, clear, and provide reasonable notice periods and conditions for cause.

risks.title: High Late Payment Penalty
risks.explanation: Payments not made within 15 days incur a significant 1.5% monthly (18% annually) late fee, and the Freelancer reserves the right to suspend work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared. This can lead to substantial additional costs and project delays.

risks.title: Limited Freelancer Liability
risks.explanation: The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement is capped at the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three months. This cap might be low relative to potential damages or losses the Client could incur due to a breach or defect.

risks.title: Non-Solicitation Clause
risks.explanation: The Client is prohibited from directly soliciting or hiring any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for 12 months after the agreement ends. This could limit the Client's ability to retain talent or build an in-house team based on project success.

risks.title: No Specific Project End Date
risks.explanation: While the project is phased, the document text does not specify an overall project completion date. This could lead to timeline ambiguities if not detailed in the 'Project Timeline & Milestones' section (Section 3) or a separate schedule.

suggestions: Review the detailed 'Project Timeline & Milestones' (Section 3) to ensure specific phase deadlines and an overall project completion date are clearly defined and agreed upon.
Negotiate the late payment penalty (1.5% per month) if it seems too high, or implement strict internal processes to ensure timely invoice payments to avoid significant additional costs.
Assess the implications of the Freelancer's liability cap and consider if it adequately covers potential risks for the Client, especially for critical systems.

Ensure a clear and detailed process for 'Change Orders' is established to manage any changes to the project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications effectively, including cost and timeline impacts.
analysis.final_advice: Before signing, ensure that the detailed project timeline and milestones are fully documented and agreed upon. Pay close attention to the payment schedule and the high late fee clause to avoid unexpected costs. Understand the scope exclusions and plan for any future needs not covered by this agreement. Clarify any ambiguities regarding project deadlines and potential impacts of the liability cap.
