content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Contract Reference No: FSA-2025-0047
Effective Date: January 10, 2025


1. PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 10, 2025, by and between:


2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to provide the following services ("Services") to the Client:
Full-stack development of a web-based SaaS platform including frontend (React.js), backend (Node.js/Express), and database architecture (PostgreSQL).
Design and implementation of RESTful APIs and third-party service integrations (Stripe, SendGrid, AWS S3).
Deployment, CI/CD pipeline setup, and cloud infrastructure configuration on AWS.
Bug fixing, testing (unit and integration), and performance optimization.
Documentation of codebase, APIs, and deployment procedures.
Any additional tasks requested by the Client's project manager at any time during the contract period, at no additional charge.

The Client reserves the right to modify, expand, or reduce the Scope of Work at any time without prior notice and without any obligation to renegotiate the compensation terms.

3. COMPENSATION & PAYMENT TERMS
3.1  Project Fee: The total fixed fee for the Services described in Section 2 is INR 85,000 (Rupees Eighty-Five Thousand Only), payable as follows:
20% (INR 17,000) upon signing of this Agreement.
80% (INR 68,000) upon final delivery and Client's written approval.

3.2  Payment Hold: The Client may withhold final payment indefinitely if, in its sole discretion, it determines that the delivered work does not meet its expectations, regardless of whether the work meets the originally agreed specifications.

3.3  Revision Requests: The Freelancer shall complete unlimited revisions requested by the Client at no additional cost, for a period of 12 months following the final delivery date.

3.4  Scope Additions: Any additional features or modules requested by the Client beyond the original scope shall be delivered by the Freelancer without additional compensation unless the Client, at its sole discretion, agrees in writing to additional payment.

3.5  Expenses: All expenses, including software licenses, third-party service fees, hosting costs, and tooling required to complete the Services, shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

3.6  Invoicing: Invoices submitted by the Freelancer will be processed within 90 (ninety) business days of Client approval. The Client shall not be held liable for delays in payment processing.

4. PROJECT TIMELINE & DEADLINES
4.1  The Freelancer agrees to deliver the complete project within 30 (thirty) calendar days from the date of this Agreement (the "Deadline").

4.2  Penalty Clause: In the event of any delay beyond the Deadline — regardless of cause, including client-side delays in feedback or approvals — the Freelancer shall be liable to pay a penalty of INR 2,000 per calendar day until delivery is completed.

4.3  The Client reserves the right to terminate this Agreement and claim a full refund of any amounts paid if the Deadline is not met, in addition to recovering any penalties accrued under Section 4.2.

4.4  Delivery of work-in-progress reports shall be provided by the Freelancer on a daily basis via email to the designated project manager, failing which the Client may apply a deduction of INR 500 per missed report from the final payment.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & OWNERSHIP
5.1  All work product, deliverables, source code, designs, documentation, ideas, concepts, inventions, and any other materials created by the Freelancer in connection with this Agreement ("Work Product") shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Client from the moment of creation, and the Freelancer waives all moral rights therein.

5.2  The Freelancer hereby assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the Work Product, including all intellectual property rights, in perpetuity, worldwide, irrevocably, and without further consideration.

5.3  Portfolio Restriction: The Freelancer shall not display, reference, discuss, or otherwise disclose the Work Product, the Client's name, or the existence of this Agreement in any portfolio, case study, blog post, social media, or professional profile without prior written consent from the Client's legal department, which may be withheld at the Client's absolute discretion.

5.4  Pre-existing Work: Any tools, libraries, or frameworks used by the Freelancer during the project, including open-source components, shall be disclosed to and approved by the Client. The Freelancer shall indemnify the Client against any intellectual property claims arising from the use of any third-party components.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
6.1  The Freelancer agrees to keep strictly confidential all proprietary information, trade secrets, business plans, client data, technical specifications, and any other information disclosed by the Client during the term of this Agreement and for a period of 7 (seven) years thereafter.

6.2  The Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, use, reproduce, or disclose any Confidential Information for any purpose other than performing the Services under this Agreement.

6.3  Breach of this section shall entitle the Client to seek injunctive relief and liquidated damages of INR 10,00,000 (Ten Lakh Rupees) per incident of disclosure, in addition to any other remedies available at law.

7. NON-COMPETE & NON-SOLICITATION
7.1  Non-Compete: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of 3 (three) years after its termination, the Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, own, manage, operate, control, or provide services to any business that is competitive with the Client's business in any jurisdiction worldwide.

7.2  Non-Solicitation: The Freelancer shall not, for a period of 3 (three) years following termination, solicit, approach, or accept work from any of the Client's existing, former, or prospective customers or business partners, whether or not the Freelancer was introduced to such parties through the Client.

7.3  Non-Hiring: The Freelancer shall not attempt to hire, recruit, or engage any employee, contractor, or vendor of the Client for a period of 3 (three) years following termination of this Agreement.

8. TERMINATION
8.1  Termination by Client: The Client may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, with or without notice. Upon termination, the Client shall owe no compensation to the Freelancer for work completed but not yet paid, and all previously paid amounts are non-refundable by the Client.

8.2  Termination by Freelancer: The Freelancer may terminate this Agreement only with 60 (sixty) days written notice and only if the Client has materially breached the Agreement and failed to cure such breach within 30 days of written notice. In all other cases, termination by the Freelancer shall be considered a breach and shall entitle the Client to recover all amounts paid as well as additional damages.

8.3  Upon termination for any reason, the Freelancer shall immediately surrender all Work Product, source code, credentials, and any other materials related to the project, and shall not retain any copies thereof.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
9.1  The Freelancer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including attorney's fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) the Freelancer's performance of the Services; (b) any breach of this Agreement by the Freelancer; (c) any third-party intellectual property claim; or (d) any negligent or wrongful act of the Freelancer.

9.2  The Client's total liability to the Freelancer under this Agreement shall be limited to INR 5,000 (Five Thousand Rupees), regardless of the nature of the claim.

9.3  The Freelancer shall maintain professional indemnity insurance of not less than INR 50,00,000 (Fifty Lakh Rupees) at its own expense during the term of this Agreement and for 2 years thereafter.

10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
10.1  This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, notwithstanding any conflict of law provisions.

10.2  Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration conducted in San Francisco, California, USA, under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (AAA). The arbitration shall be conducted in English and the costs of arbitration shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

10.3  The Freelancer waives any right to participate in a class action or class-wide arbitration against the Client.

11. MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS
11.1  Independent Contractor: The Freelancer is an independent contractor and shall not be entitled to any employee benefits, including but not limited to provident fund, gratuity, health insurance, or paid leave.

11.2  Entire Agreement: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. The Client may amend this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the Freelancer; such amendments shall become binding on the Freelancer upon sending of the notice.

11.3  No Waiver: Failure by the Client to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of its rights.

11.4  Severability: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

11.5  Force Majeure: Force majeure events shall excuse the Client from its payment obligations, but shall not excuse the Freelancer from timely delivery obligations.

11.6  Publicity: The Client may publicly reference the Freelancer's work and name in marketing materials without prior consent.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.








Contract Reference: FSA-2025-0047  |  Prepared by NexGen Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Legal Team
This document is legally binding. Please consult an independent legal advisor before signing.

analysis.overview: This contract outlines the terms for a freelancer to develop a web-based SaaS platform. It details the scope of work, payment structure, project deadlines, and intellectual property ownership. Be aware that many clauses in this agreement heavily favor the client.
analysis.plain_language_summary: This contract hires you to build a website and its backend for a company. It tells you what work you'll do, how much you'll get paid, and when to finish. However, it gives the company a lot of power to change the work, hold your pay, and own everything you create, even preventing you from showing it off.
analysis.eli15_summary: This is a job contract where you build a website for a company. It says what you have to do, how much money you'll get, and when to finish. But be super careful, because the company can change your tasks, might not pay you the full amount, and will own all your work, even your ideas, forever.
top_takeaways: The contract heavily favors the client, with many clauses that could lead to unpaid work, significant penalties, and loss of rights to your creations.
You are responsible for unlimited revisions, additional tasks, and all project expenses without guaranteed extra pay, potentially leading to a much larger workload than compensated for.
You face severe financial penalties for delays, strict restrictions on using your work in your portfolio, and a broad non-compete clause that could limit your future work for years.
analysis.duration_note: Project deadline is 30 days. Post-contract restrictions on confidentiality, non-compete, and non-solicitation apply for 3 to 7 years after termination.

key_clauses.title: Flexible Scope of Work
key_clauses.description: The client can change, add to, or reduce the work you need to do at any time without telling you beforehand or paying you more. You also have to do 'any additional tasks' requested by the client's project manager at no extra cost.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This means your workload can increase significantly without any additional payment, making it hard to manage your time, budget, and ensure fair compensation for your effort.
key_clauses.action: Clarify what 'additional tasks' means and negotiate a process for scope changes, including how they affect your fee and timeline.

key_clauses.title: Payment Terms & Hold
key_clauses.description: You'll get a total of INR 85,000. INR 17,000 (20%) when you sign, and INR 68,000 (80%) after the client approves the final work. The client can hold this final payment indefinitely if they decide the work doesn't meet their expectations, even if it matches what you originally agreed to do.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: The final 80% payment is subject to the client's 'sole discretion' and can be withheld forever, putting you at high risk of not getting paid for the majority of your work.
key_clauses.action: Insist on removing the 'sole discretion' clause or replacing it with clear, objective acceptance criteria and a defined dispute resolution process. Consider milestone-based payments.

key_clauses.title: Unlimited Revisions & Free Additions
key_clauses.description: You must complete unlimited revisions requested by the client at no additional cost for 12 months after final delivery. Also, any new features or modules requested beyond the original scope will be delivered by you without additional compensation unless the client, at their sole discretion, agrees to pay more.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This can lead to endless work without additional pay, significantly devaluing your time and effort and making it difficult to move on to other projects.
key_clauses.action: Define a reasonable number of revisions and a clear process for additional features, including how they impact cost and timeline.

key_clauses.title: Strict Deadlines & Penalties
key_clauses.description: You must finish the project within 30 calendar days. If you're late, you pay a penalty of INR 2,000 per day until delivery, even if the delay is the client's fault. The client can also end the contract, claim a full refund of any amounts paid, and still recover penalties. Missing daily reports costs INR 500 each.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This clause puts all delay risk on you, regardless of cause, and imposes severe, uncapped financial penalties, which could quickly exceed your total project fee.
key_clauses.action: Negotiate a more flexible timeline, include provisions for client-side delays, and cap any penalties at a reasonable percentage of the total fee.

key_clauses.title: Full IP Ownership by Client
key_clauses.description: The client immediately owns all your work, ideas, and code you create for this project, forever and everywhere, without paying you anything extra for the ownership itself. You also can't show this work in your portfolio or even mention the client or project without their written permission.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: You lose all rights to your creative work and cannot use it to promote your business or showcase your skills, which is a significant restriction for a freelancer.
key_clauses.action: Negotiate limited rights to display the work in your portfolio after launch, or for a specific period, with client approval on specific content.
key_clauses.value: Client owns all; No portfolio use

key_clauses.title: Confidentiality & Damages
key_clauses.description: You must keep all client information secret for 7 years after the contract ends. If you break this, the client can seek a penalty of INR 10,00,000 (Ten Lakh Rupees) per incident, plus other legal remedies.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This is a very long confidentiality period with a very high penalty for any accidental or intentional disclosure, putting you at significant financial risk.
key_clauses.action: Ensure you understand what constitutes 'Confidential Information' and have strict protocols to protect it. Consider negotiating a lower liquidated damages amount.

key_clauses.title: Broad Non-Compete & Non-Solicitation
key_clauses.description: For 3 years after the contract ends, you cannot work for any business that competes with the client anywhere in the world. You also can't contact their customers, partners, or employees to offer them work.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This severely limits your ability to work in your field after this project, potentially for a long time and globally, which could significantly impact your livelihood and career opportunities.
key_clauses.action: Try to limit the scope (geography, duration, specific types of competing businesses) or remove this clause entirely, as it's very restrictive for a freelancer.

key_clauses.title: Client's Easy Termination
key_clauses.description: The client can end this contract whenever they want, for any reason, even without telling you. If they do, they don't have to pay you for any work you've completed but haven't been paid for yet. Your ability to terminate is very restricted, and doing so without cause is a breach, allowing the client to recover all amounts paid and additional damages.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: You could invest significant time and effort into the project only for the client to terminate without paying you for your completed work, leaving you with no recourse.
key_clauses.action: Negotiate for payment for all work completed up to the termination date, regardless of who terminates or the reason for termination.

payment.summary: The total fixed fee is INR 85,000. You receive INR 17,000 (20%) upon signing and INR 68,000 (80%) upon final delivery and the client's written approval. Invoices will be processed within 90 business days of client approval. The client can withhold the final payment indefinitely if they determine the work doesn't meet their expectations, even if it meets original specifications.
payment.risk: High. The client has the right to indefinitely withhold 80% of your payment at their 'sole discretion,' creating a significant risk of not being paid for most of your work. Payment processing also takes a very long 90 business days.

work_scope.summary: You are to provide full-stack development for a web-based SaaS platform, including frontend, backend, database, APIs, deployment, testing, and documentation. The client can modify, expand, or reduce this scope at any time without notice or renegotiating your compensation. You must also perform 'any additional tasks' requested by the client's project manager at no extra charge and complete unlimited revisions for 12 months after delivery.
work_scope.risk: High. The scope is highly flexible at the client's discretion, meaning your workload can increase significantly without additional compensation. The requirement for 'unlimited revisions' for 12 months and 'any additional tasks' without extra pay are major risks for scope creep and unpaid work.

ownership.summary: All work product, code, designs, ideas, and any other materials you create for this project become the client's sole and exclusive property from the moment of creation, worldwide, forever, and without any additional payment for this ownership. You are also explicitly forbidden from displaying, referencing, or discussing the work, the client's name, or the agreement in your portfolio or any public forum without prior written consent from the client's legal department.
ownership.risk: High. You lose all intellectual property rights to your creative work and cannot use it to promote your business or showcase your skills, which is a significant drawback for a freelancer who relies on a portfolio.

deadlines.summary: You must deliver the complete project within 30 calendar days from the agreement date. If there's any delay, regardless of the cause (even client-side delays), you are liable to pay a penalty of INR 2,000 per calendar day until delivery. The client can also terminate the agreement, claim a full refund of any amounts paid, and recover any accrued penalties. Missing daily work-in-progress reports will result in a INR 500 deduction from your final payment per missed report.
deadlines.risk: High. This clause imposes an extremely tight deadline with severe, uncapped financial penalties for any delay, regardless of who is at fault. The client can also claim a full refund and penalties, making this a very risky clause.

ending_terms.summary: The client can terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, with or without notice. If they terminate, they owe you no compensation for work completed but not yet paid. You can only terminate with 60 days' written notice and only if the client has materially breached the agreement and failed to fix it within 30 days. If you terminate for any other reason, it's considered a breach, and the client can recover all amounts paid and additional damages.
ending_terms.risk: High. The client has an extremely easy exit without paying for your completed work, while your ability to terminate is severely restricted and comes with significant financial penalties if not done under very specific conditions.

risks.title: Unpaid Work & Indefinite Payment Hold
risks.explanation: The client can withhold 80% of your payment indefinitely if they decide the work doesn't meet their expectations, even if it aligns with the original specifications. This means you might not get paid for the majority of your effort.
risks.what_to_do: Negotiate clear, objective acceptance criteria for deliverables and milestone payments. Insist on removing the 'sole discretion' clause for payment approval.

risks.title: Unlimited Work & Scope Creep
risks.explanation: You are obligated to perform 'any additional tasks' and 'unlimited revisions' for 12 months without extra pay. The client can also modify the scope at will without renegotiating compensation, potentially leading to a significantly increased workload for the same fixed fee.
risks.what_to_do: Define a clear, fixed scope of work. Negotiate a limited number of revisions and a formal change order process for any additional tasks or scope changes that includes revised timelines and compensation.

risks.title: Severe Penalties for Delays
risks.explanation: You face a penalty of INR 2,000 per day for any delay beyond 30 days, even if the client causes the delay. The client can also terminate the contract, get a full refund, and still collect penalties, which could quickly exceed your total project fee.
risks.what_to_do: Negotiate a more realistic timeline, include provisions for client-caused delays, and cap any penalties at a reasonable percentage of the total fee.

risks.title: Loss of Intellectual Property & Portfolio Rights
risks.explanation: The client owns everything you create from the start, and you cannot use any of your work in your portfolio or even mention the client or project without their explicit permission. This prevents you from showcasing your skills for future work.
risks.what_to_do: Negotiate for the right to display the work in your portfolio after the project launch, perhaps with client approval on specific screenshots or descriptions.

risks.title: Broad Non-Compete & Non-Solicitation
risks.explanation: For 3 years after the contract ends, you cannot work for any competitor worldwide or solicit the client's customers, partners, or employees. This severely limits your ability to work in your field after this project.
risks.what_to_do: Try to limit the geographic scope (e.g., specific cities/states), duration, and definition of 'competitive business' to something reasonable for your industry, or seek to remove this clause.

suggestions: Seek independent legal counsel to review the highly unfavorable clauses before signing.
Negotiate significant changes to the payment, scope, penalty, intellectual property, and non-compete clauses to protect your interests.
Carefully consider if the fixed fee of INR 85,000 adequately compensates for the extensive risks, potential for unlimited work, and severe post-contract restrictions.
action_items: Negotiate the 'sole discretion' payment hold clause to include clear, objective acceptance criteria for deliverables.
Clarify the definition of 'additional tasks' and 'unlimited revisions' and propose a formal change order process for any scope changes.
Propose a cap on daily penalties and include provisions to account for client-side delays in the project timeline.
Request the right to display the completed work in your professional portfolio after the project launches, with client approval on specific content.
Review the non-compete clause with a legal expert to understand its enforceability and potential impact on your future work opportunities.
questions_to_ask: Can we define objective criteria for 'Client's written approval' for the final payment, instead of 'sole discretion'?
What is the formal process for scope changes, and how will additional features or tasks affect the project fee and timeline?
Can the daily penalty for delays be capped at a reasonable percentage of the total fee, and can we include provisions for delays caused by client feedback or approvals?
Would it be possible to include a clause allowing me to showcase the completed work in my professional portfolio after the project launches, perhaps with specific content approved by you?
analysis.final_advice: This agreement contains many clauses that are highly unfavorable to a freelancer, potentially leading to unpaid work, unlimited scope, severe penalties, and significant restrictions on your future career. It's crucial to negotiate these terms before signing, as they could have a major impact on your financial stability and professional freedom. Do not sign this agreement as is.
analysis.confidence_note: The document text was clear and complete.

stats.duration_note: Project deadline is 30 days. Post-contract restrictions on confidentiality, non-compete, and non-solicitation apply for 3 to 7 years after termination.

content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.overview: This agreement outlines the terms for a freelancer to develop a web application for a client's HR system. It covers the project scope, payment terms, intellectual property, confidentiality, and how changes or termination will be handled.
analysis.plain_language_summary: This is a contract where a freelancer agrees to build a web app for a company. It details what needs to be built, how much the freelancer will be paid, who owns the final code, and rules about keeping information secret. It also explains what happens if the project changes or ends early.
analysis.eli15_summary: This paper is a deal between you (the freelancer) and a company. You'll build a website part for them. It says what you'll make, how much money you'll get, who owns the website code, and rules for keeping secrets. It also tells you what to do if things change or the project stops.
top_takeaways: You will be paid INR 2,00,000 for the project, with a 1.5% monthly late fee if the client doesn't pay on time.
The client will own the custom web application code once you've been fully paid for each phase.
Your financial responsibility (liability) is capped at the total fees paid to you in the last three months, which protects you from large claims.
analysis.duration_note: The agreement is for a specific project, not a fixed time period beyond project completion.

key_clauses.title: Project Scope & Deliverables
key_clauses.description: This section clearly lists what you need to build, like a custom employee dashboard and a notification system. It also explicitly states what is NOT included, such as mobile app development or ongoing support after the warranty.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This prevents 'scope creep,' where the client asks for more work than agreed without extra pay. Knowing what's out of scope helps manage expectations.
key_clauses.action: Carefully review both the 'Deliverables' and 'Out of Scope' lists to ensure they match your understanding and what you discussed.

key_clauses.title: Payment & Late Fees
key_clauses.description: You will be paid a total of INR 2,00,000, excluding taxes. Payments are due within 15 days of invoicing each milestone. If payments are late, a penalty of 1.5% per month (18% per year) will be charged on the overdue amount.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This ensures you get paid fairly and on time. The late fee provides an incentive for the client to pay promptly.
key_clauses.action: Confirm the payment schedule for each milestone and ensure your bank details are correctly listed in Schedule A.

key_clauses.title: Intellectual Property Ownership
key_clauses.description: Once you receive full payment for each phase, the client will own all the custom code, designs, and documents you create specifically for this project. You can still use general tools or show anonymized project screenshots in your portfolio.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This defines who has the rights to the work you create. It means you can't reuse the exact custom code for another client, but you can still showcase your skills.
key_clauses.action: Understand that the client will own the specific project code. Keep track of any pre-existing tools you use that you want to retain rights to.
key_clauses.value: Client owns custom work upon full payment

key_clauses.title: Confidentiality
key_clauses.description: Both you and the client agree to keep all private business information secret. You cannot share client data or use it for anything other than this project. This obligation lasts for three years even after the project ends.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: Protecting the client's sensitive information is crucial. Breaching confidentiality can lead to serious legal issues.
key_clauses.action: Be extremely careful with any client data or business secrets. Delete client data from your systems once the project is complete and no longer needed.

key_clauses.title: Revisions & Change Requests
key_clauses.description: Each milestone includes up to two rounds of minor revisions at no extra cost, as long as they don't change the original plan. Any major changes to the project scope will require a written 'Change Order' that specifies new costs and timelines.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This protects you from endless free revisions and ensures you are compensated for any significant extra work requested by the client.
key_clauses.action: Always get any changes to the project scope in writing through a formal Change Order before starting the extra work.
key_clauses.value: 2 minor revisions/milestone; Change Order for scope changes

key_clauses.title: Termination
key_clauses.description: Either party can end the agreement with 30 days' written notice. If the client ends it, they must pay you for all work completed up to that date. Either party can also terminate immediately for serious breaches, like not fixing a problem within 14 days.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This provides a clear exit strategy for both sides. It ensures you get paid for the work you've already done, even if the project stops early.
key_clauses.action: If the agreement is terminated, ensure you submit an invoice for all completed work and understand what constitutes a 'material breach'.

key_clauses.title: Limitation of Liability
key_clauses.description: Your total financial responsibility (liability) under this agreement is limited to the total fees the client paid you in the three months before a claim. Neither party is responsible for indirect damages like lost revenue or data.
key_clauses.why_it_matters: This is a significant protection for you, limiting your financial risk if something goes wrong with the software you deliver. It prevents you from being liable for huge, unforeseen losses.
key_clauses.action: Be aware of this cap. It's generally a favorable clause for a freelancer.

payment.summary: You will receive a total of INR 2,00,000 for the project, paid in installments upon completion of each milestone. Payments are due within 15 days of invoicing. If payments are late, a penalty of 1.5% per month will be added. You are responsible for your own income taxes, and GST will be charged additionally if applicable.
payment.risk: Medium. While there's a good late payment penalty, client payment delays can still impact your cash flow. Ensure you track invoices and due dates closely.

work_scope.summary: You are responsible for designing, developing, testing, and delivering specific web application components for the client's HR system, including an employee dashboard, API integration, and a reporting module. Explicitly excluded are mobile app development, third-party payroll integrations, and ongoing support beyond the warranty period.
work_scope.risk: Low. The scope is clearly defined with specific deliverables and exclusions, which helps prevent misunderstandings and 'scope creep'.

ownership.summary: The client will own all the custom code, designs, and documentation created specifically for this project once you have received full payment for each phase. You retain rights to general tools, frameworks, and to display anonymized project screenshots in your portfolio.
ownership.risk: Medium. While standard for 'work made for hire,' it means you cannot directly reuse the specific custom code for other clients, which might limit future opportunities for direct code reuse.

deadlines.summary: The project will be completed in four phases, but specific milestone dates are not provided in this document. Time extensions due to client delays in feedback or approvals will not count against your timeline.
deadlines.risk: Medium. Lack of specific milestone dates in the document could lead to ambiguity. Client delays, even if not counted against you, can still disrupt your schedule and other commitments.

ending_terms.summary: Either party can end the agreement with 30 days' written notice. If the client terminates, they must pay you for all work completed up to that point. Immediate termination is possible for serious breaches, like not fixing a problem within 14 days.
ending_terms.risk: Low. The terms are clear and provide a reasonable notice period. You are protected by the clause ensuring payment for completed work upon termination.

risks.title: Client Payment Delays
risks.explanation: Even with a late payment penalty of 1.5% per month, if the client frequently delays payments, it can significantly impact your cash flow and ability to manage your finances.
risks.what_to_do: Invoice promptly upon milestone completion and follow up immediately if payment is not received within the 15-day window.

risks.title: Scope Creep Without Compensation
risks.explanation: While the 'Out of Scope' section is helpful, clients might still request minor changes that, over time, add up to significant extra work without a formal Change Order and additional payment.
risks.what_to_do: Document all client requests, even minor ones, and clearly communicate when a request falls outside the agreed scope and requires a Change Order.

risks.title: Limited Reuse of Custom Code
risks.explanation: Since the client owns the custom code upon full payment, you cannot directly reuse that specific code for other clients, which might mean more work starting from scratch on future projects.
risks.what_to_do: Ensure you have a library of general-purpose tools and frameworks that you developed independently and can reuse across projects, as allowed by the 'Freelancer's Retained Rights' clause.

risks.title: Confidentiality Breach
risks.explanation: Accidentally or intentionally disclosing client's proprietary information could lead to legal action, especially given the 3-year survival clause.
risks.what_to_do: Implement strict data handling practices, use secure communication channels, and delete all client confidential data once the project is complete and no longer needed.

suggestions: Create a detailed project plan with specific dates for each milestone and share it with the client.
Maintain clear communication logs for all project discussions, decisions, and change requests.
Set up a system to track invoices, payments, and due dates to proactively manage your cash flow.
action_items: Confirm the specific start and end dates for each of the four project phases with the client.
Set up a clear process for submitting and approving Change Orders for any scope modifications.
Ensure your bank details in Schedule A are accurate for NEFT/RTGS transfers.
Clarify the process for submitting receipts for reimbursable expenses and the expected turnaround for payment.
questions_to_ask: Can we establish specific dates for each of the four project phases and milestones to ensure alignment?
What is the preferred method for submitting Change Orders, and what is the typical approval timeline?
Who is the primary point of contact for feedback and approvals to avoid delays?
What is the expected warranty period for the delivered software after launch?
analysis.final_advice: This is a well-structured agreement with good protections for you, especially regarding liability and late payments. Focus on clear communication, meticulous documentation of scope changes, and timely invoicing to ensure a smooth project. Always get significant changes in writing.
analysis.confidence_note: The document text provided was complete and clear for analysis.

stats.duration_note: The agreement is for a specific project, not a fixed time period beyond project completion.

content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.  
 
4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule  
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The 
Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the 
corresponding deliverable.

Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are

4.4 Expenses

The Project may incorporate open -source libraries subjec t to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The 
Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third -party dependencies in 
Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open -source components carry their respective

11.3 Force Majeure  
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including

11.4 Entire Agreement  
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement

11.5 Severability

3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.
