4.3 Late Payment Penalty  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 3 of 7 Payments not received within the 15 -day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per 
annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The 
Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are 
cleared.  
 
4.4 Expenses  
Any pre -approved third -party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurre d by the 
Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon 
presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.  
 
4.5 Taxes  
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law 
to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, 
the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged 
additionally on all invoices.  
 
 
5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS  
5.1 Work Made for Hire  
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created 
exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shal l hold 
all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.  
 
5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights  
The Freelancer retains the right to:  
• Utilize any general -purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this 
Agreement  
• Display a nonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio  
• Retain pre -existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre -existing IP")  
 
5.3 Open Source Components  
The Project may incorporate open -source libraries subjec t to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The 
Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third -party dependencies in 
Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open -source components carry their respective 
license obligat ions.  
 
 FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 4 of 7 6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON -DISCLOSURE  
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and 
business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:  
• Disclose any Client data, b usiness logic, or system architecture to third parties  
• Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this 
Agreement  
• Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project 
requirements  
 
This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) 
years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the 
Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was alread y in possession of prior to disclosure.  
 
 
7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS  
7.1 Included Revisions  
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, 
provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter 
the originally agreed scope.  
 
7.2 Scope Changes  
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via 
a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shal l specify the additional cost, timeline 
impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change 
requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.  
 
 
8. TERMINATION  
8.1 Termination for  Convenience  
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of 
Client -initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, 
calculated on a pro -rata basis agai nst the milestone payments.  
 
8.2 Termination for Cause  
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) 
materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving  
written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful 
misconduct.  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 5 of 7  
8.3 Effect of Termination  
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current 
state.  Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding 
invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.  
 
 
9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION  
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in 
the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, 
incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to l oss of revenue, loss of data, or business 
interruption.  
 
The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal 
costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions  to 
incorporate third -party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the 
Client.  
 
 
10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW  
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes 
arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good -faith 
negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.  
 
If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matt er to binding 
arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, 
before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.  
 
 
11. GENERAL PROVISIONS  
11.1 Indep endent Contractor  
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The 
Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self -
employment taxes and statutory  dues.  
 
11.2 Non -Solicitation  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 6 of 7 During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to 
directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the 
purposes of this Project.  
 
11.3 Force Majeure  
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including 
natural disasters, government actions, pandemic -related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, 
provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.  
 
11.4 Entire Agreement  
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement 
between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to 
the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.  
 
11.5 Severability  
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue 
in full force and effect.  
 
 
12. SIGNATURES  
By signing below, b oth parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by 
the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

Web Application Development Project  
Contract No.  FSA-2025 -0047 Effective Date  May 1, 2025  
 
 
1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT  
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between 
the following parties:  
 
CLIENT  
 
NovaSpark Technologies Pvt. Ltd.

12B, Tower C, Prestige Business Park,

Karnataka, India

West Bengal, India  
Email: arjun.dev@protonmail.com  
PAN: BXAPS7821K  
 
 
2. SCOPE OF WORK  
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components 
for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":  
 
2.1 Deliverables  
• Custom Employee Dashboard with role -based access control (RBAC)  
• RESTful API integration with the Client's exist ing PostgreSQL database  
• Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS  
• Real-time notification system using WebSockets  
• PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll  
• Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management  
• Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)  
• Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support  
 
2.2 Out of Scope  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 2 of 7 The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written  
amendment or a new agreement if required:  
• Mobile application development (iOS/Android)  
• Third -party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)  
• Ongoing post -launch technical support beyond the warranty period  
• Any hardware procurement or server  infrastructure costs  
 
 
3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES  
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to 
Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.  
 
# Phase / Deliverable  Scope Summary  Deadline  Payment (INR)  
1 Phase 1 – Discovery & 
Architecture  Requirements analysis, system 
design docs, DB schema, API 
contracts  May 15, 2025  ₹30,000  
2 Phase 2 – Core 
Development  Dashboard, RBAC, API 
integration, database layer  June 15, 2025  ₹75,000  
3 Phase 3 – Advanced 
Features  Real-time notifications, PDF 
module, admin panel  July 10, 2025  ₹60,000  
4 Phase 4 – Testing & 
Deployment  QA, documentation, Docker 
config, AWS deployment  July 31, 2025  ₹35,000  
TOTAL PROJECT VALUE  ₹2,00,000  
 
 
4. PAYMENT TERMS  
4.1 Compensation  
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of 
applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to 
the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.  
 
4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule  
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The 
Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the 
corresponding deliverable.  
 
4.3 Late Payment Penalty  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 3 of 7 Payments not received within the 15 -day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per 
annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The 
Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are 
cleared.  
 
4.4 Expenses  
Any pre -approved third -party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurre d by the 
Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon 
presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.  
 
4.5 Taxes  
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law 
to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, 
the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged 
additionally on all invoices.  
 
 
5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS  
5.1 Work Made for Hire  
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created

all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.  
 
5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights  
The Freelancer retains the right to:  
• Utilize any general -purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this 
Agreement  
• Display a nonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio  
• Retain pre -existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre -existing IP")  
 
5.3 Open Source Components  
The Project may incorporate open -source libraries subjec t to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The

license obligat ions.

Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and 
business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:  
• Disclose any Client data, b usiness logic, or system architecture to third parties  
• Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this 
Agreement  
• Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project 
requirements  
 
This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) 
years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the 
Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was alread y in possession of prior to disclosure.  
 
 
7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS  
7.1 Included Revisions  
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, 
provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter 
the originally agreed scope.  
 
7.2 Scope Changes  
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via 
a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shal l specify the additional cost, timeline 
impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change 
requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.

Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of 
Client -initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, 
calculated on a pro -rata basis agai nst the milestone payments.  
 
8.2 Termination for Cause  
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) 
materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving  
written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful 
misconduct.  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 5 of 7  
8.3 Effect of Termination  
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current 
state.  Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding 
invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.  
 
 
9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION  
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in 
the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, 
incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to l oss of revenue, loss of data, or business 
interruption.  
 
The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal 
costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions  to 
incorporate third -party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the 
Client.  
 
 
10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW  
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes 
arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good -faith 
negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.  
 
If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matt er to binding

before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.  
 
 
11. GENERAL PROVISIONS  
11.1 Indep endent Contractor  
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The

employment taxes and statutory  dues.

Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including

This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement

If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue

12. SIGNATURES  
By signing below, b oth parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by

Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 1 of 7 FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  
Web Application Development Project  
Contract No.  FSA-2025 -0047 Effective Date  May 1, 2025  
 
 
1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT  
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between 
the following parties:  
 
CLIENT  
 
NovaSpark Technologies Pvt. Ltd.  
Attn: Mr. Rajiv Menon, CTO  
12B, Tower C, Prestige Business Park,  
Outer Ring Road, Bangalore – 560103,  
Karnataka, India  
Email: rajiv.menon@novaspark.io  
GSTIN: 29AADCN1234F1Z5  FREELANCER  
 
Arjun Sharma  
Independent Software Developer  
Flat 402, Green Valley Apartments,  
Sector 15, Siliguri – 734001,  
West Bengal, India  
Email: arjun.dev@protonmail.com  
PAN: BXAPS7821K  
 
 
2. SCOPE OF WORK  
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components 
for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":  
 
2.1 Deliverables  
• Custom Employee Dashboard with role -based access control (RBAC)  
• RESTful API integration with the Client's exist ing PostgreSQL database  
• Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS  
• Real-time notification system using WebSockets  
• PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll  
• Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management  
• Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)  
• Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support  
 
2.2 Out of Scope  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 2 of 7 The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written  
amendment or a new agreement if required:  
• Mobile application development (iOS/Android)  
• Third -party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)  
• Ongoing post -launch technical support beyond the warranty period  
• Any hardware procurement or server  infrastructure costs  
 
 
3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES  
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to 
Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.  
 
# Phase / Deliverable  Scope Summary  Deadline  Payment (INR)  
1 Phase 1 – Discovery & 
Architecture  Requirements analysis, system 
design docs, DB schema, API 
contracts  May 15, 2025  ₹30,000  
2 Phase 2 – Core 
Development  Dashboard, RBAC, API 
integration, database layer  June 15, 2025  ₹75,000  
3 Phase 3 – Advanced 
Features  Real-time notifications, PDF 
module, admin panel  July 10, 2025  ₹60,000  
4 Phase 4 – Testing & 
Deployment  QA, documentation, Docker 
config, AWS deployment  July 31, 2025  ₹35,000  
TOTAL PROJECT VALUE  ₹2,00,000  
 
 
4. PAYMENT TERMS  
4.1 Compensation  
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of 
applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to 
the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.  
 
4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule  
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The 
Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the 
corresponding deliverable.  
 
4.3 Late Payment Penalty  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 3 of 7 Payments not received within the 15 -day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per 
annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The 
Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are 
cleared.  
 
4.4 Expenses  
Any pre -approved third -party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurre d by the 
Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon 
presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.  
 
4.5 Taxes  
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law 
to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, 
the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged 
additionally on all invoices.  
 
 
5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS  
5.1 Work Made for Hire  
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created 
exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shal l hold 
all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.  
 
5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights  
The Freelancer retains the right to:  
• Utilize any general -purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this 
Agreement  
• Display a nonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio  
• Retain pre -existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre -existing IP")  
 
5.3 Open Source Components  
The Project may incorporate open -source libraries subjec t to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The 
Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third -party dependencies in 
Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open -source components carry their respective 
license obligat ions.  
 
 FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 4 of 7 6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON -DISCLOSURE  
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and 
business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:  
• Disclose any Client data, b usiness logic, or system architecture to third parties  
• Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this 
Agreement  
• Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project 
requirements  
 
This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) 
years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the 
Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was alread y in possession of prior to disclosure.  
 
 
7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS  
7.1 Included Revisions  
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, 
provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter 
the originally agreed scope.  
 
7.2 Scope Changes  
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via 
a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shal l specify the additional cost, timeline 
impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change 
requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.  
 
 
8. TERMINATION  
8.1 Termination for  Convenience  
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of 
Client -initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, 
calculated on a pro -rata basis agai nst the milestone payments.  
 
8.2 Termination for Cause  
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) 
materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving  
written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful 
misconduct.  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 5 of 7  
8.3 Effect of Termination  
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current 
state.  Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding 
invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.  
 
 
9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION  
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in 
the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, 
incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to l oss of revenue, loss of data, or business 
interruption.  
 
The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal 
costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions  to 
incorporate third -party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the 
Client.  
 
 
10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW  
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes 
arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good -faith 
negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.  
 
If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matt er to binding 
arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, 
before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.  
 
 
11. GENERAL PROVISIONS  
11.1 Indep endent Contractor  
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The 
Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self -
employment taxes and statutory  dues.  
 
11.2 Non -Solicitation  FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 6 of 7 During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to 
directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the 
purposes of this Project.  
 
11.3 Force Majeure  
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including 
natural disasters, government actions, pandemic -related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, 
provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.  
 
11.4 Entire Agreement  
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement 
between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to 
the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.  
 
11.5 Severability  
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue 
in full force and effect.  
 
 
12. SIGNATURES  
By signing below, b oth parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by 
the terms and conditions of this Agreement.  
 
 
FOR CLIENT  
NovaSpark Technologies Pvt. Ltd.  
 
 
 
 
 
Authorized Signatory  
Name: Mr. Rajiv Menon  
Title: Chief Technology Officer  
Date: ____________________   FREELANCER  
Arjun Sharma, Independent Developer  
 
 
 
 
 
Authorized Signatory  
Name: Arjun Sharma  
Title: Freelance Developer  
Date: ____________________  
 
 FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT  |  Confidential  |  Page 7 of 7 SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT  
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:  
 
Account Holder Name  Arjun Sharma  
Bank Name  HDFC Bank Ltd.  
Account Number  5020 0198 3456 72  
IFSC Code  HDFC0001247  
Branch  Siliguri Main Branch, West Bengal  
Account Type  Current Account  
UPI ID  arjunsharma.dev@hdfcbank  
 
Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for 
NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Contract Reference No: FSA-2025-0047
Effective Date: January 10, 2025


1. PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 10, 2025, by and between:


2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to provide the following services ("Services") to the Client:
Full-stack development of a web-based SaaS platform including frontend (React.js), backend (Node.js/Express), and database architecture (PostgreSQL).
Design and implementation of RESTful APIs and third-party service integrations (Stripe, SendGrid, AWS S3).
Deployment, CI/CD pipeline setup, and cloud infrastructure configuration on AWS.
Bug fixing, testing (unit and integration), and performance optimization.
Documentation of codebase, APIs, and deployment procedures.
Any additional tasks requested by the Client's project manager at any time during the contract period, at no additional charge.

The Client reserves the right to modify, expand, or reduce the Scope of Work at any time without prior notice and without any obligation to renegotiate the compensation terms.

3. COMPENSATION & PAYMENT TERMS
3.1  Project Fee: The total fixed fee for the Services described in Section 2 is INR 85,000 (Rupees Eighty-Five Thousand Only), payable as follows:
20% (INR 17,000) upon signing of this Agreement.
80% (INR 68,000) upon final delivery and Client's written approval.

3.2  Payment Hold: The Client may withhold final payment indefinitely if, in its sole discretion, it determines that the delivered work does not meet its expectations, regardless of whether the work meets the originally agreed specifications.

3.3  Revision Requests: The Freelancer shall complete unlimited revisions requested by the Client at no additional cost, for a period of 12 months following the final delivery date.

3.4  Scope Additions: Any additional features or modules requested by the Client beyond the original scope shall be delivered by the Freelancer without additional compensation unless the Client, at its sole discretion, agrees in writing to additional payment.

3.5  Expenses: All expenses, including software licenses, third-party service fees, hosting costs, and tooling required to complete the Services, shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

3.6  Invoicing: Invoices submitted by the Freelancer will be processed within 90 (ninety) business days of Client approval. The Client shall not be held liable for delays in payment processing.

4. PROJECT TIMELINE & DEADLINES
4.1  The Freelancer agrees to deliver the complete project within 30 (thirty) calendar days from the date of this Agreement (the "Deadline").

4.2  Penalty Clause: In the event of any delay beyond the Deadline — regardless of cause, including client-side delays in feedback or approvals — the Freelancer shall be liable to pay a penalty of INR 2,000 per calendar day until delivery is completed.

4.3  The Client reserves the right to terminate this Agreement and claim a full refund of any amounts paid if the Deadline is not met, in addition to recovering any penalties accrued under Section 4.2.

4.4  Delivery of work-in-progress reports shall be provided by the Freelancer on a daily basis via email to the designated project manager, failing which the Client may apply a deduction of INR 500 per missed report from the final payment.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & OWNERSHIP
5.1  All work product, deliverables, source code, designs, documentation, ideas, concepts, inventions, and any other materials created by the Freelancer in connection with this Agreement ("Work Product") shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Client from the moment of creation, and the Freelancer waives all moral rights therein.

5.2  The Freelancer hereby assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the Work Product, including all intellectual property rights, in perpetuity, worldwide, irrevocably, and without further consideration.

5.3  Portfolio Restriction: The Freelancer shall not display, reference, discuss, or otherwise disclose the Work Product, the Client's name, or the existence of this Agreement in any portfolio, case study, blog post, social media, or professional profile without prior written consent from the Client's legal department, which may be withheld at the Client's absolute discretion.

5.4  Pre-existing Work: Any tools, libraries, or frameworks used by the Freelancer during the project, including open-source components, shall be disclosed to and approved by the Client. The Freelancer shall indemnify the Client against any intellectual property claims arising from the use of any third-party components.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
6.1  The Freelancer agrees to keep strictly confidential all proprietary information, trade secrets, business plans, client data, technical specifications, and any other information disclosed by the Client during the term of this Agreement and for a period of 7 (seven) years thereafter.

6.2  The Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, use, reproduce, or disclose any Confidential Information for any purpose other than performing the Services under this Agreement.

6.3  Breach of this section shall entitle the Client to seek injunctive relief and liquidated damages of INR 10,00,000 (Ten Lakh Rupees) per incident of disclosure, in addition to any other remedies available at law.

7. NON-COMPETE & NON-SOLICITATION
7.1  Non-Compete: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of 3 (three) years after its termination, the Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, own, manage, operate, control, or provide services to any business that is competitive with the Client's business in any jurisdiction worldwide.

7.2  Non-Solicitation: The Freelancer shall not, for a period of 3 (three) years following termination, solicit, approach, or accept work from any of the Client's existing, former, or prospective customers or business partners, whether or not the Freelancer was introduced to such parties through the Client.

7.3  Non-Hiring: The Freelancer shall not attempt to hire, recruit, or engage any employee, contractor, or vendor of the Client for a period of 3 (three) years following termination of this Agreement.

8. TERMINATION
8.1  Termination by Client: The Client may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, with or without notice. Upon termination, the Client shall owe no compensation to the Freelancer for work completed but not yet paid, and all previously paid amounts are non-refundable by the Client.

8.2  Termination by Freelancer: The Freelancer may terminate this Agreement only with 60 (sixty) days written notice and only if the Client has materially breached the Agreement and failed to cure such breach within 30 days of written notice. In all other cases, termination by the Freelancer shall be considered a breach and shall entitle the Client to recover all amounts paid as well as additional damages.

8.3  Upon termination for any reason, the Freelancer shall immediately surrender all Work Product, source code, credentials, and any other materials related to the project, and shall not retain any copies thereof.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
9.1  The Freelancer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including attorney's fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) the Freelancer's performance of the Services; (b) any breach of this Agreement by the Freelancer; (c) any third-party intellectual property claim; or (d) any negligent or wrongful act of the Freelancer.

9.2  The Client's total liability to the Freelancer under this Agreement shall be limited to INR 5,000 (Five Thousand Rupees), regardless of the nature of the claim.

9.3  The Freelancer shall maintain professional indemnity insurance of not less than INR 50,00,000 (Fifty Lakh Rupees) at its own expense during the term of this Agreement and for 2 years thereafter.

10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
10.1  This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, notwithstanding any conflict of law provisions.

10.2  Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration conducted in San Francisco, California, USA, under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (AAA). The arbitration shall be conducted in English and the costs of arbitration shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

10.3  The Freelancer waives any right to participate in a class action or class-wide arbitration against the Client.

11. MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS
11.1  Independent Contractor: The Freelancer is an independent contractor and shall not be entitled to any employee benefits, including but not limited to provident fund, gratuity, health insurance, or paid leave.

11.2  Entire Agreement: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. The Client may amend this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the Freelancer; such amendments shall become binding on the Freelancer upon sending of the notice.

11.3  No Waiver: Failure by the Client to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of its rights.

11.4  Severability: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

11.5  Force Majeure: Force majeure events shall excuse the Client from its payment obligations, but shall not excuse the Freelancer from timely delivery obligations.

11.6  Publicity: The Client may publicly reference the Freelancer's work and name in marketing materials without prior consent.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.








Contract Reference: FSA-2025-0047  |  Prepared by NexGen Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Legal Team
This document is legally binding. Please consult an independent legal advisor before signing.

analysis.summary: This document is not a contract; it only contains a name and a certification title. Because of this, all crucial details about your work, payment, and rights are completely missing. There are no terms about how much you'll be paid, what work you need to do, or who owns what you create. This means you have no legal protection or clear expectations for this project. It's extremely risky to proceed without a proper agreement.

Make sure the contract clearly states your payment amount, when you'll get paid, and what happens if payments are late.

Clarify who owns the work you create and how you can use it.
Define clear deadlines for your work and for client feedback.
Understand how the contract can be ended by either side.

content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Contract Reference No: FSA-2025-0047
Effective Date: January 10, 2025


1. PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 10, 2025, by and between:


2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to provide the following services ("Services") to the Client:
Full-stack development of a web-based SaaS platform including frontend (React.js), backend (Node.js/Express), and database architecture (PostgreSQL).
Design and implementation of RESTful APIs and third-party service integrations (Stripe, SendGrid, AWS S3).
Deployment, CI/CD pipeline setup, and cloud infrastructure configuration on AWS.
Bug fixing, testing (unit and integration), and performance optimization.
Documentation of codebase, APIs, and deployment procedures.
Any additional tasks requested by the Client's project manager at any time during the contract period, at no additional charge.

The Client reserves the right to modify, expand, or reduce the Scope of Work at any time without prior notice and without any obligation to renegotiate the compensation terms.

3. COMPENSATION & PAYMENT TERMS
3.1  Project Fee: The total fixed fee for the Services described in Section 2 is INR 85,000 (Rupees Eighty-Five Thousand Only), payable as follows:
20% (INR 17,000) upon signing of this Agreement.
80% (INR 68,000) upon final delivery and Client's written approval.

3.2  Payment Hold: The Client may withhold final payment indefinitely if, in its sole discretion, it determines that the delivered work does not meet its expectations, regardless of whether the work meets the originally agreed specifications.

3.3  Revision Requests: The Freelancer shall complete unlimited revisions requested by the Client at no additional cost, for a period of 12 months following the final delivery date.

3.4  Scope Additions: Any additional features or modules requested by the Client beyond the original scope shall be delivered by the Freelancer without additional compensation unless the Client, at its sole discretion, agrees in writing to additional payment.

3.5  Expenses: All expenses, including software licenses, third-party service fees, hosting costs, and tooling required to complete the Services, shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

3.6  Invoicing: Invoices submitted by the Freelancer will be processed within 90 (ninety) business days of Client approval. The Client shall not be held liable for delays in payment processing.

4. PROJECT TIMELINE & DEADLINES
4.1  The Freelancer agrees to deliver the complete project within 30 (thirty) calendar days from the date of this Agreement (the "Deadline").

4.2  Penalty Clause: In the event of any delay beyond the Deadline — regardless of cause, including client-side delays in feedback or approvals — the Freelancer shall be liable to pay a penalty of INR 2,000 per calendar day until delivery is completed.

4.3  The Client reserves the right to terminate this Agreement and claim a full refund of any amounts paid if the Deadline is not met, in addition to recovering any penalties accrued under Section 4.2.

4.4  Delivery of work-in-progress reports shall be provided by the Freelancer on a daily basis via email to the designated project manager, failing which the Client may apply a deduction of INR 500 per missed report from the final payment.

5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & OWNERSHIP
5.1  All work product, deliverables, source code, designs, documentation, ideas, concepts, inventions, and any other materials created by the Freelancer in connection with this Agreement ("Work Product") shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Client from the moment of creation, and the Freelancer waives all moral rights therein.

5.2  The Freelancer hereby assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the Work Product, including all intellectual property rights, in perpetuity, worldwide, irrevocably, and without further consideration.

5.3  Portfolio Restriction: The Freelancer shall not display, reference, discuss, or otherwise disclose the Work Product, the Client's name, or the existence of this Agreement in any portfolio, case study, blog post, social media, or professional profile without prior written consent from the Client's legal department, which may be withheld at the Client's absolute discretion.

5.4  Pre-existing Work: Any tools, libraries, or frameworks used by the Freelancer during the project, including open-source components, shall be disclosed to and approved by the Client. The Freelancer shall indemnify the Client against any intellectual property claims arising from the use of any third-party components.

6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
6.1  The Freelancer agrees to keep strictly confidential all proprietary information, trade secrets, business plans, client data, technical specifications, and any other information disclosed by the Client during the term of this Agreement and for a period of 7 (seven) years thereafter.

6.2  The Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, use, reproduce, or disclose any Confidential Information for any purpose other than performing the Services under this Agreement.

6.3  Breach of this section shall entitle the Client to seek injunctive relief and liquidated damages of INR 10,00,000 (Ten Lakh Rupees) per incident of disclosure, in addition to any other remedies available at law.

7. NON-COMPETE & NON-SOLICITATION
7.1  Non-Compete: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of 3 (three) years after its termination, the Freelancer shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, own, manage, operate, control, or provide services to any business that is competitive with the Client's business in any jurisdiction worldwide.

7.2  Non-Solicitation: The Freelancer shall not, for a period of 3 (three) years following termination, solicit, approach, or accept work from any of the Client's existing, former, or prospective customers or business partners, whether or not the Freelancer was introduced to such parties through the Client.

7.3  Non-Hiring: The Freelancer shall not attempt to hire, recruit, or engage any employee, contractor, or vendor of the Client for a period of 3 (three) years following termination of this Agreement.

8. TERMINATION
8.1  Termination by Client: The Client may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, with or without notice. Upon termination, the Client shall owe no compensation to the Freelancer for work completed but not yet paid, and all previously paid amounts are non-refundable by the Client.

8.2  Termination by Freelancer: The Freelancer may terminate this Agreement only with 60 (sixty) days written notice and only if the Client has materially breached the Agreement and failed to cure such breach within 30 days of written notice. In all other cases, termination by the Freelancer shall be considered a breach and shall entitle the Client to recover all amounts paid as well as additional damages.

8.3  Upon termination for any reason, the Freelancer shall immediately surrender all Work Product, source code, credentials, and any other materials related to the project, and shall not retain any copies thereof.

9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
9.1  The Freelancer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including attorney's fees) arising out of or relating to: (a) the Freelancer's performance of the Services; (b) any breach of this Agreement by the Freelancer; (c) any third-party intellectual property claim; or (d) any negligent or wrongful act of the Freelancer.

9.2  The Client's total liability to the Freelancer under this Agreement shall be limited to INR 5,000 (Five Thousand Rupees), regardless of the nature of the claim.

9.3  The Freelancer shall maintain professional indemnity insurance of not less than INR 50,00,000 (Fifty Lakh Rupees) at its own expense during the term of this Agreement and for 2 years thereafter.

10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
10.1  This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, notwithstanding any conflict of law provisions.

10.2  Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration conducted in San Francisco, California, USA, under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (AAA). The arbitration shall be conducted in English and the costs of arbitration shall be borne entirely by the Freelancer.

10.3  The Freelancer waives any right to participate in a class action or class-wide arbitration against the Client.

11. MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS
11.1  Independent Contractor: The Freelancer is an independent contractor and shall not be entitled to any employee benefits, including but not limited to provident fund, gratuity, health insurance, or paid leave.

11.2  Entire Agreement: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. The Client may amend this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the Freelancer; such amendments shall become binding on the Freelancer upon sending of the notice.

11.3  No Waiver: Failure by the Client to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not be construed as a waiver of its rights.

11.4  Severability: If any provision is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

11.5  Force Majeure: Force majeure events shall excuse the Client from its payment obligations, but shall not excuse the Freelancer from timely delivery obligations.

11.6  Publicity: The Client may publicly reference the Freelancer's work and name in marketing materials without prior consent.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.








Contract Reference: FSA-2025-0047  |  Prepared by NexGen Solutions Pvt. Ltd. Legal Team
This document is legally binding. Please consult an independent legal advisor before signing.

content:
FREELANCE SERVICES AGREEMENT
Web Application Development Project


1. PARTIES TO THIS AGREEMENT
This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 1, 2025, by and between the following parties:



2. SCOPE OF WORK
The Freelancer agrees to design, develop, test, and deliver the following web application components for the Client's internal HR management system, hereinafter referred to as the "Project":

2.1 Deliverables
Custom Employee Dashboard with role-based access control (RBAC)
RESTful API integration with the Client's existing PostgreSQL database
Responsive UI/UX design using React.js (v18+) and Tailwind CSS
Real-time notification system using WebSockets
PDF report generation module for monthly attendance and payroll
Admin panel with full CRUD operations for user management
Comprehensive unit and integration test suite (minimum 80% code coverage)
Deployment configuration scripts for AWS EC2 with Docker support

2.2 Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this Agreement and will require a separate written amendment or a new agreement if required:
Mobile application development (iOS/Android)
Third-party payroll gateway integrations (e.g., RazorpayX, Cashfree)
Ongoing post-launch technical support beyond the warranty period
Any hardware procurement or server infrastructure costs


3. PROJECT TIMELINE & MILESTONES
The Project shall be executed in four (4) phases as outlined in the table below. Time extensions due to Client delays in feedback or approvals shall not be counted against the Freelancer.



4. PAYMENT TERMS
4.1 Compensation
The total compensation for the Project is Two Lakh Indian Rupees (INR 2,00,000), exclusive of applicable taxes. All payments shall be made in Indian Rupees (INR) via NEFT/RTGS bank transfer to the account details provided by the Freelancer in Schedule A of this Agreement.

4.2 Invoicing & Payment Schedule
The Freelancer shall raise invoices upon completion of each milestone as defined in Section 3. The Client shall make payment within fifteen (15) calendar days of receiving the invoice and approving the corresponding deliverable.

4.3 Late Payment Penalty
Payments not received within the 15-day window shall incur a late fee of 1.5% per month (18% per annum) on the outstanding amount, calculated from the due date until receipt of full payment. The Freelancer reserves the right to suspend active work on subsequent phases until overdue invoices are cleared.

4.4 Expenses
Any pre-approved third-party costs (e.g., AWS credits, licensed fonts, stock assets) incurred by the Freelancer solely for the Project shall be reimbursed by the Client within seven (7) days upon presentation of valid receipts, separate from the project fee.

4.5 Taxes
The Freelancer is responsible for payment of all applicable income taxes. If the Client is required by law to withhold TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) under Section 194C or 194J of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the Client shall issue Form 16A within the statutory timeline. GST, if applicable, shall be charged additionally on all invoices.


5. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
5.1 Work Made for Hire
Upon receipt of full payment for each Phase, all custom code, designs, and documentation created exclusively for this Project shall be assigned to the Client as a work made for hire. The Client shall hold all rights, title, and interest in the final delivered work.

5.2 Freelancer's Retained Rights
The Freelancer retains the right to:
Utilize any general-purpose libraries, frameworks, or tools developed independently prior to this Agreement
Display anonymized screenshots or descriptions of the Project in a professional portfolio
Retain pre-existing proprietary code, utilities, and development tools ("Pre-existing IP")

5.3 Open Source Components
The Project may incorporate open-source libraries subject to MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD licenses. The Freelancer shall provide a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) listing all third-party dependencies in Phase 1 deliverables. The Client acknowledges that open-source components carry their respective license obligations.


6. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE
Both parties agree to maintain in strict confidence all proprietary information, trade secrets, and business data disclosed during the term of this Agreement. The Freelancer shall not:
Disclose any Client data, business logic, or system architecture to third parties
Use confidential information for any purpose outside of fulfilling obligations under this Agreement
Copy or retain Client databases, employee records, or financial data beyond project requirements

This confidentiality obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years. Exceptions apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Freelancer, or information the Freelancer was already in possession of prior to disclosure.


7. REVISIONS & CHANGE REQUESTS
7.1 Included Revisions
Each milestone deliverable includes up to two (2) rounds of minor revisions at no additional cost, provided the revision request is submitted within seven (7) calendar days of delivery and does not alter the originally agreed scope.

7.2 Scope Changes
Any changes to the Project scope, deliverables, or technical specifications must be agreed in writing via a Change Order signed by both parties. Change Orders shall specify the additional cost, timeline impact, and revised deliverables. The Freelancer is under no obligation to begin work on change requests until a signed Change Order and any required advance payment are received.


8. TERMINATION
8.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) calendar days' written notice. In the event of Client-initiated termination, the Client shall pay for all work completed up to the termination date, calculated on a pro-rata basis against the milestone payments.

8.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if the other party: (a) materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving written notice; (b) becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy; or (c) commits fraud or engages in willful misconduct.

8.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination, the Freelancer shall deliver all completed work and associated files in their current state. Ownership of completed deliverables shall transfer to the Client upon payment of all outstanding invoices. Work in progress shall remain property of the Freelancer until payment is received.


9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY & INDEMNIFICATION
The Freelancer's total liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the preceding three (3) months. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, consequential, incidental, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of revenue, loss of data, or business interruption.

The Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Freelancer from any claims, damages, or legal costs arising from: (a) the Client's misuse of the delivered software; (b) the Client's instructions to incorporate third-party content without proper licensing; or (c) any breach of this Agreement by the Client.


10. DISPUTE RESOLUTION & GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes arising from this Agreement shall first be attempted to be resolved amicably through good-faith negotiations within thirty (30) days of written notice of the dispute.

If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation, the parties agree to submit the matter to binding arbitration in Bangalore, Karnataka, administered under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, before a sole arbitrator mutually appointed by both parties. The language of arbitration shall be English.


11. GENERAL PROVISIONS
11.1 Independent Contractor
The Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, or agent of the Client. The Freelancer shall have no authority to bind the Client contractually and shall be responsible for all self-employment taxes and statutory dues.

11.2 Non-Solicitation
During the term of this Agreement and for twelve (12) months thereafter, the Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire any subcontractors or team members introduced by the Freelancer for the purposes of this Project.

11.3 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be held liable for delays caused by events beyond their reasonable control, including natural disasters, government actions, pandemic-related restrictions, or internet infrastructure failures, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice.

11.4 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, together with any Schedules and Change Orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior discussions, representations, or agreements relating to the Project. Any amendment must be in writing and signed by both parties.

11.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.


12. SIGNATURES
By signing below, both parties acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.




SCHEDULE A – BANK DETAILS FOR PAYMENT
The Client shall remit all payments to the following bank account of the Freelancer:


Note: The Client is solely responsible for any transaction charges levied by the Client's bank for NEFT/RTGS transfers. All bank details above are for domestic INR transfers only.

analysis.summary: This contract outlines a web application development project where you'll build several components for a client's HR system. It details the specific work, how you'll get paid in milestones, and rules about ownership and confidentiality. However, crucial information like who the parties are and the exact project timeline with deadlines is completely missing. This makes it risky to sign without getting those important details filled in first.

risks.title: Missing Client & Freelancer Details
risks.explanation: The contract doesn't say who the client or the freelancer actually are, which means you don't know who you're legally agreeing with.
risks.description: The contract doesn't say who the client or the freelancer actually are, which means you don't know who you're legally agreeing with.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the other term before signing.

risks.title: No Project Timeline or Milestones
risks.explanation: There are no specific dates or deadlines for when each part of the project needs to be done, making it hard to plan your work and know when payments are due.
risks.description: There are no specific dates or deadlines for when each part of the project needs to be done, making it hard to plan your work and know when payments are due.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the work term before signing.

risks.title: No Upfront Payment
risks.explanation: The contract doesn't mention any initial payment or advance, meaning you might start working without any money upfront.
risks.description: The contract doesn't mention any initial payment or advance, meaning you might start working without any money upfront.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the payment term before signing.

risks.title: Client Approval for Payments
risks.explanation: Your payment for each milestone depends on the client approving your work, which could delay your money if they take a long time to review.
risks.description: Your payment for each milestone depends on the client approving your work, which could delay your money if they take a long time to review.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the payment term before signing.

risks.title: Work Made for Hire
risks.explanation: Everything you create specifically for this project will fully belong to the client, meaning you give up all ownership rights to that specific code and design.
risks.description: Everything you create specifically for this project will fully belong to the client, meaning you give up all ownership rights to that specific code and design.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the ownership term before signing.

risks.title: Limited Revisions
risks.explanation: You only get two rounds of minor changes per milestone, and anything more or outside the original plan will likely cost extra or require a new agreement.
risks.description: You only get two rounds of minor changes per milestone, and anything more or outside the original plan will likely cost extra or require a new agreement.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the work term before signing.

risks.title: Client Can End Anytime
risks.explanation: The client can end the project with 30 days' notice for any reason, even if you haven't finished, though they will pay for work completed up to that point.
risks.description: The client can end the project with 30 days' notice for any reason, even if you haven't finished, though they will pay for work completed up to that point.
risks.what_to_do: Ask to clarify or improve the termination term before signing.

risks.title: Missing Bank Details
risks.explanation: Your bank details in Schedule A are not filled in, so the client won't know where to send your payments.
risks.description: Your bank details in Schedule A are not filled in, so the client won't know where to send your payments.
